disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden severe unilateral flank/back pain radiating anteriorly towards the groin and testicle
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the pain and does it move anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's in one side of my back and flank and it shoots round to the front, down towards my groin. It came on all of a sudden and woke me up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Extreme physical restlessness — pacing and writhing, cannot lie still
trigger_question_examples: Is it easier to lie still or do you feel you have to move?
What are you doing to cope with the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't lie still at all — I have to keep pacing and rolling around, no position helps.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intractable vomiting with complete inability to tolerate oral fluids or medication
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to keep any fluids down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I keep throwing up — I can't keep water or the paracetamol down at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Single episode of macroscopic haematuria just before the pain peaked
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any blood in your urine?
What colour has your urine been?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I saw blood in it once, just before the pain got really bad.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, rigors or dysuria reported prior to the acute event (infection-screen negatives)
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, shivering or shakes?
Any burning or stinging when you pass urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or shivering that I noticed, and no stinging when I wee before this all started.
